title: Randomized Algorithms, Approximation, and Honest Guarantees · Scale Transfer
summary: Choose scalable methods when exact optimization is too expensive or adversarial. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a probabilistic and approximation guarantee report with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish Las Vegas and Monte Carlo guarantees, failure probability, and expected cost.
Analyze random pivot selection against input-order adversaries and seeded tests.
Reduce independent error probability through repeated trials and justified aggregation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
